title: Add checkpoint/resume durability to sprint orchestration (LangGraph-style)
source: LangGraph multi-agent supervisor + checkpointer/durable-state docs (from prior knowledge; live fetch blocked)
description: LangGraph's core contribution over ad-hoc orchestration is a persisted graph state with checkpointing so a long multi-agent run can be interrupted and resumed without redoing completed work. The orchestrator already writes SESSION_STATE.json, but sprint execution in orchestrate.py appears to run tickets in a single pass — a crash or ctrl-C mid-sprint loses in-flight progress. Persisting per-ticket execution state (queued/running/review/done + last handoff) after each step makes sprints resumable and idempotent.
proposed_changes: In session.py add `checkpoint_ticket(ticket_id, phase, handoff)` and `load_sprint_checkpoint()` that persist an execution ledger (e.g. .cto/session/sprint_checkpoint.json) atomically after every phase transition. In orchestrate.py, before running a ticket check the checkpoint and skip tickets already marked done; on start of each phase (delegate/review/close) write the checkpoint. Add a `--resume` flag to the sprint entrypoint that replays from the last checkpoint instead of re-delegating completed tickets.
